pattern: An edge is critical exactly when it is a bridge, so Tarjan's low-link depth-first search flags every edge whose subtree cannot reach back above its parent.
hints: A connection matters only if removing it disconnects the graph, which is the definition of a bridge.
Track each node's discovery time and the earliest discovery time reachable from its subtree via back edges.
During DFS, an edge (u, v) is a bridge when the lowest reachable time from v's subtree is strictly greater than u's discovery time; collect all such edges.

edge_inputs.description: a cycle with one pendant edge, only the pendant is critical
single edge is itself a bridge
a full cycle has no bridges at all
a path graph where every edge is a bridge
two cycles joined by a single bridge

stress.description: a random spanning tree plus a few extra edges so some connections are bridges and some are not
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    edges = []
    seen = set()
    for v in range(1, n):
        u = rng.randrange(0, v)
        edges.append([u, v])
        seen.add(frozenset((u, v)))
    extra = rng.randrange(0, n)
    tries = 0
    while extra > 0 and tries < 10 * n:
        tries += 1
        a = rng.randrange(0, n)
        b = rng.randrange(0, n)
        if a == b:
            continue
        k = frozenset((a, b))
        if k in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(k)
        edges.append([a, b])
        extra -= 1
    rng.shuffle(edges)
    return [n, edges]
stress.note: spanning tree guarantees connectivity; extra chords remove some bridges
